facebook.content:
You know, marriage is a school of ministry. What you sow in your marriage, you'll harvest outside of it. This is why I always say, don't try to change your husband or your wife; change your heart, and the way you treat them. 

The Bible says, "As a man thinks in his heart, so is he." (Proverbs 23:7) What you think about yourself, your spouse, and your marriage determines what you'll experience.
facebook.fullPost:

The Bible says, "As a man thinks in his heart, so is he." (Proverbs 23:7) What you think about yourself, your spouse, and your marriage determines what you'll experience.

facebook.pullQuote: What you sow in your marriage, you'll harvest outside of it.

instagram.content:
Your Identity Dictates Your Harvest

You are not who you've been through or what you've endured.
You are who God says you are.

Psalm 30:5: "Weeping may last through the night, but joy comes with the morning."

If you've been through a storm, don't define yourself by the storm.
You are a child of God, redeemed, and loved.

Double tap if you're ready to see yourself through God's eyes.

tiktok.content: You think you're responsible for fixing your marriage, but scripture says: "A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you; and I will take the stony heart out of your flesh, and give you a heart of flesh." Ezekiel 36:26.
